7. WHEN all reconnection attempts are exhausted and the Website is operating in HTTP polling fallback mode, THE Website SHALL display a persistent indicator informing the user that realtime updates are degraded

### Requirement 16: Database and Data Architecture

**User Story:** As a system architect, I want a well-structured database supporting all game ecosystem features with proper relationships and indexing, so that queries perform efficiently at scale.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Player_Service SHALL persist user data in PostgreSQL with tables for: users, characters, guilds, items, inventory, marketplace_listings, territories, rankings, news, notifications, and audit_logs, with foreign key constraints enforcing referential integrity between related tables
2. THE Ranking_Service SHALL cache computed rankings in Redis with a time-to-live of 60 seconds to reduce database load
3. THE Player_Service SHALL use Prisma ORM for all database operations with type-safe query generation
4. THE Analytics_Service SHALL store time-series metrics with a retention period of 90 days, supporting range queries that return results within 2 seconds for any single-dashboard visualization request spanning up to 30 days of data
5. THE Marketplace_Service SHALL implement database transactions for all trade operations to ensure atomicity of item and currency transfers
6. IF a database transaction fails during a trade operation, THEN THE Marketplace_Service SHALL roll back all changes, preserve the original state of both parties' inventories and currency balances, and return an error message indicating the failure reason
7. THE Player_Service SHALL define database indexes on all columns used in frequent query patterns including: user lookup by email, character lookup by user ID, inventory lookup by character ID, marketplace listings by item and status, and rankings by category and score
8. THE Analytics_Service SHALL partition time-series data by time interval to support efficient deletion of records older than the 90-day retention period without impacting query performance on active data

### Requirement 17: Download and Launcher Integration

**User Story:** As a new player, I want to download the game launcher easily with clear instructions and progress feedback, so that I can start playing quickly.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Website SHALL provide a dedicated Download page with launcher download links for Windows operating system, displaying the installer file size in megabytes (MB) or gigabytes (GB)
2. THE Website SHALL display system requirements on the Download page in both minimum and recommended tiers, covering the following categories: operating system version, CPU, RAM, GPU, and available storage space
3. THE Website SHALL display the current game version number and total patch size (in MB or GB) on the Download page
4. WHEN a player clicks the download button, THE Website SHALL initiate the launcher installer download and display a post-download instruction guide containing numbered steps for: running the installer, completing installation, and launching the game
5. THE Website SHALL provide at least 2 download sources (mirrors or CDN-backed links) selectable by the player to allow fallback if one source is unavailable
6. IF the download link is unavailable or returns an error, THEN THE Website SHALL display an error message indicating the download failure and prompt the player to select an alternative download source
7. THE Website SHALL apply Neo Brutalism styling to the Download page with thick borders on download cards and hard offset shadows on action buttons

### Requirement 18: Guild System

**User Story:** As a guild leader, I want to manage my guild through the website including member management, territory claims, and guild communication, so that I can coordinate guild activities outside the game.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Guild_Service SHALL support guild profile pages displaying guild name, emblem, description (maximum 500 characters), member count, territory count, and creation date
2. THE Guild_Service SHALL support member roster display with member name, rank, class, level, last online time, and contribution score, paginated at a maximum of 50 members per page
3. THE Guild_Service SHALL support guild search and browsing with filters for minimum level, territory count, and recruitment status, returning a maximum of 20 results per page with pagination controls
4. WHEN a guild leader updates guild settings (guild name, description, emblem, or recruitment status) through the Player_Dashboard, THE Guild_Service SHALL apply changes within 5 seconds and display a confirmation indicator to the guild leader
5. THE Website SHALL display a public guilds directory page with guild cards styled in Neo Brutalism with thick borders and guild emblem display, showing a maximum of 20 guild cards per page
6. WHEN a guild leader promotes, demotes, or removes a member through the Player_Dashboard, THE Guild_Service SHALL apply the membership change within 5 seconds and notify the affected member via the Notification_Service
7. IF a non-leader member attempts a guild management action they do not have permission for, THEN THE Guild_Service SHALL reject the action and display an error message indicating insufficient guild rank permissions
8. THE Guild_Service SHALL support a guild activity feed on the Player_Dashboard displaying member join/leave events, territory war results, and leader announcements, showing the 50 most recent entries

### Requirement 19: Support and Help System

**User Story:** As a player experiencing issues, I want access to support resources and ticket submission, so that I can resolve problems with my account or gameplay.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Website SHALL provide a Support page with FAQ section, knowledge base search, and ticket submission form requiring the following fields: support category, subject (maximum 150 characters), description (maximum 2000 characters), and optional file attachments (up to 3 files, each no larger than 5 MB)
2. WHEN a player submits a support ticket with all required fields completed, THE Website SHALL confirm submission with a unique ticket reference number and display the estimated response time for the selected category
3. IF a player submits a support ticket with one or more required fields empty or exceeding length limits, THEN THE Website SHALL display a validation error indicating which fields need correction and SHALL preserve all entered data
4. THE Website SHALL categorize support topics into: Account Issues, Technical Problems, Billing, Bug Reports, and General Inquiries
5. THE Website SHALL display up to 50 most recent support tickets in the Player_Dashboard for authenticated players, showing for each ticket: reference number, subject, category, submission date, and current status (Open, In Progress, Awaiting Response, or Resolved)
6. IF a player is not authenticated, THEN THE Website SHALL allow ticket submission with a valid email address for response delivery
7. WHEN a player searches the knowledge base and no results match the query, THE Website SHALL display a no-results message and suggest submitting a support ticket

### Requirement 20: Monorepo Architecture and Deployment

**User Story:** As a developer, I want a well-organized monorepo structure with shared packages and automated deployment, so that the codebase is maintainable and deployable independently per application.

#### Acceptance Criteria

1. THE Website SHALL be organized as a monorepo with application directories: apps/web (public frontend), apps/api (backend services), apps/admin (admin panel), and apps/launcher (desktop launcher)
2. THE Website SHALL share code through package directories: packages/ui (shared components), packages/auth (authentication utilities), packages/configs (shared configurations), packages/sdk (API client SDK), packages/database (Prisma schema and migrations), and packages/shared (common types and utilities)
3. THE Website SHALL deploy the frontend application to Vercel with automatic preview deployments on every pull request targeting the main branch
4. THE Website SHALL deploy the backend application via Docker containers on VPS with NGINX reverse proxy
5. WHEN a pull request is merged to the main branch, THE Website SHALL execute a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that runs linting, type-checking, and tests for affected applications, and proceeds to build and deploy only if all checks pass
6. IF the CI/CD pipeline fails during testing or building, THEN THE Website SHALL halt deployment and report the failure status on the corresponding GitHub commit
7. THE Website SHALL use Docker Compose for local development environment setup, providing PostgreSQL and Redis services that start within 60 seconds and are accessible to all applications in the monorepo
8. WHEN a shared package is modified, THE Website SHALL rebuild and redeploy only the applications that depend on that package
</content>
